title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Failure Triage
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a capability and surface decision matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.
Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
Match reasoning effort and autonomy to task complexity, reversibility, and verification cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: For Surface Fit, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-provider-portability-1sl221" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Provider Portability into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-provider-portability-1sl221
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Provider Portability precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Provider Portability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.
